Context: This chunk provides an overview of the Indian real estate market for May 2025, published by JLL in their "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report. It summarizes key developments in real estate investment, new business ventures, policy updates, and market indices for office, retail, and residential sectors across major cities.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 2

# Overview

### Realestonomics
ASK Property Fund and India Sotheby’s International Realty (ISIR) backed real estate fund has raised INR 500 cr to invest in luxury housing projects across major cities within three months of the launch of its Alternative Investment Fund platform.

### Market sentiment
**Favourable**
(Sentiment scale: Favourable, Neutral, Unfavourable)

### What’s new
Bengaluru-based real estate major Sumadhura Group has forayed into the managed office space business with Workship, under which it aims to lease over a million square feet and more than 10,000 seats over the next three years.

### Policy update
Chennai Unified Metropolitan Transport Authority (CUMTA) is devising a 25-year Comprehensive Mobility Plan for Chennai Metropolitan Area with the key aim of efficient, seamless public transport for citizens.

### Indices
*   **Office Index:** Demand for office space increased in most cities
*   **Retail Index:** Retail space take-up in shopping malls increased in most cities
*   **Residential Index:** New launches went up in most cities

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk is from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report, published in May 2025. It provides an in-depth analysis of India's retail market dynamics for Q1 2025, covering demand, supply, and leasing trends across the top 7 cities.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 3

# Focus sector: Retail
## India Retail Market Dynamics Q1 2025

**Retail sector surges with 169% YoY growth in Q1 2025 as gross leasing of 3.1 million sq. ft. is witnessed across top 7 cities**

India’s retail sector continued to demonstrate impressive performance in terms of both demand and supply in the first quarter of calendar year 2025. While demand for retail spaces continued unabated across the top 7 cities, the infusion of new retail spaces also totaled to a new high. During Q1 2025, 3.1 million sq. ft was leased by retailers in shopping malls and high streets across India’s top 7 cities. On the supply front, Q1 2025 witnessed new retail space additions of 2 million sq. ft.

Retail leasing activity witnessed a significant year-on-year (YoY) growth of 169% as strong momentum in new retail store openings continued to play out in major metropolitan cities. On a quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) basis, gross leasing activity in the retail sector noted an uptrend of 9%. In terms of geographic spread of leasing volume, Bengaluru and Hyderabad together accounted for 60% of the overall India leasing activity. Prominent high streets across both these cities witnessed vibrant leasing in suburban micro-markets with larger store size requirements coming from retailer categories such as daily needs and grocery, food and beverages and fashion and apparel.

Amongst retailer categories, fashion and apparel continued to remain the frontrunner in India’s retail landscape. The segment accounted for a significant 31% share of the leasing activity in Q1 2025. Retailers in the food & beverage segment comprised 21% share of the leasing pie followed by entertainment at 16%. During the quarter, domestic retailers continued to lead retail space take-up with an 86% share. With growing proliferation of direct to consumer (D2C) brands in the offline space and expansion by domestic fashion anchors, the share of domestic retailers in the gross leasing activity has been rising steadily. This quarter also saw 8 new brands of international origin open their first retail store in the country.

Over the next three quarters (April-December 2025), 7 million sq. ft. of new Grade A supply of shopping malls is expected as per the current delivery timelines. With this upcoming new supply, the retail sector’s leasing is expected to remain healthy on the back of demand expansion from retailers due to availability of quality spaces in prime micro-markets in key Indian cities.

**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS, India, JLL
samantak.das@jll.com

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: This chunk from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' for May 2025 provides a detailed overview of the real estate market in Ahmedabad. It covers sector summaries for office, retail, and residential, including key transactions, launches, city highlights, and market data on rental and capital values.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 5

# Ahmedabad

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Demand for office space remain stagnant.
    *   **Key transaction:** Veolia at GIFT City
*   **Retail:** Demand in malls and high-streets remain stable.
    *   **Key transaction:** Libas at Vastrapur
*   **Residential:** Sales and launches remain robust.
    *   **Key launch:** Skyzenia at Thaltej

### City highlights
The Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation (AMC) has initiated a new round of registrations for nine properties with a substantial cumulative base price of INR 920 cr. Among these offerings are two premium commercial parcels situated in proximity to Sindhu Bhavan Road, which together have been valued at INR 333 cr.

### Infrastructure/policy update
No major infrastructure or policy updates were recorded.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (sq ft/month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 40 - 60 |
| Retail | 100 - 140 |
| Residential | 20 - 30 |

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/sq ft)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 10,000 |
| Retail | 25,000 - 30,000 |
| Residential | 6,000 - 8,000 |

**Caption:** Capital Value (INR/sq ft) in Ahmedabad. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

Context: This chunk, from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' for May 2025, provides a detailed overview of the real estate market dynamics in Bangalore. It covers sector summaries for office, retail, and residential, along with city highlights, infrastructure updates, and market data on rental and capital values.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 6

# Bengaluru

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Outer Ring Road and Whitefield witnessed most of the leasing traction.
    *   **Key transaction:** Alstom Transport India at Banaswadi
*   **Retail:** Leasing activity was strong in high streets, in home furnishing and fashion and apparel segments.
    *   **Key transaction:** Apple at Hebbal
*   **Residential:** Hosa Road and Sarjapur Road in the south and Devanahalli in north Bengaluru saw most of the launches.
    *   **Key launch:** Sattva Vasanta Skye at Devanahalli.

### City highlights
*   Brigade Group has acquired 11 acres of land parcel near ITPL to develop a commercial project with a Gross Development Value (GDV) of INR 2,000 Cr.
*   Puravankara Group and KVN Property Holdings have partnered in a joint venture to develop a 24.59-acre land parcel in North Bengaluru.
*   Sumadhura Group forays into managed office spaces sector with Workship; spans over 0.12 mn sq ft and offers more than 3,000 seats.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Bengaluru Development Authority (BDA) recently increased property tax by 20% - 45%.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (INR/ sq ft/ month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 100 - 140 |
| Retail | 60 - 180 |
| Residential | 20 - 40 |

| Sector | Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 14,000 - 18,000 |
| Retail | 14,000 - 20,000 |
| Residential | 14,000 - 18,000 |

**Caption:** Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month) in Bengaluru. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 7

# Chennai

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Sustained leasing momentum led by BFSI and Manufacturing firms in the SBD OMR, SBD & CBD micromarkets.
    *   **Key transaction:** AT&T at OMR
*   **Retail:** Prominent high streets and malls led the leasing.
    *   **Key transaction:** Birkenstock at Royapettah
*   **Residential:** New launches and sales activity remain muted.
    *   **Key launch:** Brigade Morgan Heights at Perumbakam

### City highlights
*   Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin on Wednesday inaugurated two manufacturing plants, set up with a total investment of INR 475 cr.
*   Royal Enfield opens its fourth Manufacturing plant in Chennai spread over 60 acres with an investment of INR 1,000 cr.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Chennai Unified Metropolitan Transport Authority (CUMTA) is devising a 25-year Comprehensive Mobility Plan for Chennai Metropolitan Area with the key aim of efficient, seamless public transport for citizens.

### Market Data

| Residential | 16,000 - 20,000 |

Context: This chunk is part of the "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor May 2025" report published by JLL in June 2025. It provides a detailed overview of the current real estate market dynamics for the Office, Retail, and Residential sectors specifically within Delhi NCR.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 8

# Delhi NCR

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Office space take-up remained strong.
    *   **Key transaction:** ABP News at Noida-Greater Noida Expressway
*   **Retail:** Healthy retail leasing activity across Delhi NCR.
    *   **Key transaction:** Jaypore at Golf Course Road Extension
*   **Residential:** Launches and sales remain strong.
    *   **Key launch:** Godrej Majesty at Sector 12, Greater Noida

### City highlights
*   County Group has acquired 13.3 acres of land in Wave City NH-24, Ghaziabad for ~ INR 400 cr. The land lies within the 4,200-acre smart city project developed by Wave Group, strategically located adjacent to NH-24.
*   Prestige Estates Projects Ltd recorded over ₹3,000 crore in sales within a week of launching its first residential project in the NCR. The project, The Prestige City, located in Indirapuram, sold 1,200 units during the initial launch phase.
*   Smartworld Developers and Tribeca Developers announced the successful sale of all 298 Trump branded residences in Gurugram, securing INR 3,250 cr in a single day.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Greater Noida Authority has unveiled plans for a 4 km elevated road connecting Ek Murti Chowk in Greater Noida West to NH-9 via Shahberi and Crossings Republik. The proposed six-lane corridor will link Greater Noida, Greater Noida West, Ghaziabad, and Delhi through the Delhi-Meerut Expressway.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 250 |
| Retail | 150 - 250 |
| Residential | 20 - 50 |

    *   **Key launch:** Godrej Majesty at Sector 12, Greater Noida

### City highlights
*   County Group has acquired 13.3 acres of land in Wave City NH-24, Ghaziabad for ~ INR 400 cr. The land lies within the 4,200-acre smart city project developed by Wave Group, strategically located adjacent to NH-24.
*   Prestige Estates Projects Ltd recorded over ₹3,000 crore in sales within a week of launching its first residential project in the NCR. The project, The Prestige City, located in Indirapuram, sold 1,200 units during the initial launch phase.
*   Smartworld Developers and Tribeca Developers announced the successful sale of all 298 Trump branded residences in Gurugram, securing INR 3,250 cr in a single day.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Greater Noida Authority has unveiled plans for a 4 km elevated road connecting Ek Murti Chowk in Greater Noida West to NH-9 via Shahberi and Crossings Republik. The proposed six-lane corridor will link Greater Noida, Greater Noida West, Ghaziabad, and Delhi through the Delhi-Meerut Expressway.

### Market Data

| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 250 |
| Retail | 150 - 250 |
| Residential | 20 - 50 |

**Caption:** Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) in Delhi NCR. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft)* |
| :--- | :--- |

| Retail | 20,000 - 25,000 |

Context: This chunk is from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report, published in June 2025, focusing on the real estate market dynamics for May 2025. It specifically details the Hyderabad market, providing sector summaries, city highlights, infrastructure updates, and market data for office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 9

# Hyderabad

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Healthy leasing traction in Kokapet and Financial District, Gachibowli.
    *   **Key transaction:** Capgemini at Kokapet
*   **Retail:** Anchor brands contribute to retail leasing momentum.
    *   **Key transaction:** Pantaloons at Chandanagar
*   **Residential:** Property launches and sales continue at a steady pace.
    *   **Key launch:** ASBL Broadway at Pappulguda

### City highlights
*   Tribeca Developers has partnered with Ira realty to bring Trump Towers in Kokapet. The project cost is around INR 3,500 cr. It is expected to be ready for possession by 2030.
*   TCS leased over 1 mn sq ft of office space at Rajapushpa Paradigm building in the Financial District, Gachibowli.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority (HDMA) is currently developing land pooling initiatives of totaling approximately 300 acres are currently being developed in Pratap Singaram, Lemur and Inmulnarva(near kothur) to boost revenue.

Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor May 2025" provides a detailed overview of the real estate market in Kolkata and Mumbai. It includes sector summaries for office, retail, and residential, along with key transactions, launches, city highlights, infrastructure updates, and market data for rental and capital values in both cities.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 10

# Kolkata

### Sector Summaries
*   **Office:** Office market showed a constant demand in May.
    *   **Key transaction:** KPMG at Salt Lake
*   **Retail:** High Street Retail reflected healthy demand.
    *   **Key transaction:** Style Baazar at Esplanade
*   **Residential:** New launches remained strong across micro markets.
    *   **Key launch:** Merlin Avana Phase-II at Haridevpur

### City highlights
*   Merlin Projects has launched a Residential project Merlin Avana Phase II at Haridevpur near Tollygunge.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Housing Infrastructure Development Corporation (HIDCO) has earmarked a 9-acre plot near Kadampukur in the border area of New Town AA II to get a wildlife conservation park.

# Mumbai

### Office
The Eastern Suburbs had the most leasing activity compared to all other submarkets.
**Key transaction:** IFF at Powai

### Retail
Demand for malls and highstreets were robust.
**Key transaction:** Mr. D.I.Y at Borivali

### Residential
Sales and launches remain stagnant.
**Key launch:** Sejal City in Goregaon East

Kanakia Group is collaborating with Hines, Mitsubishi, and Sumitomo on a 1.5 mn sq ft luxury office development in Mumbai's BKC.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Metro line 3 services from BKC to Acharya Atre Chowk (Worli) begins.

Context: This chunk is from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" report, published in May 2025. It provides a detailed overview of the current real estate market performance in Pune across the office, retail, and residential sectors.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor

# Pune

### Office
Leasing activity primarily driven by IT & ITeS in the SBD submarket.
**Key transaction:** Amdocs at Hadapsar

### Retail
Highstreet across the city has seen strong leasing activity.
**Key transaction:** Frido at Viman Nagar

### Residential
Sales and Launches remain stable.
**Key launch:** Mayfair River Residence at Mundhwa

Godrej Properties acquired 14 acres of land in Kharadi to develop a housing project, with a potential developable area of 3.7 mn sq ft. The company aims to generate a revenue of INR 4,200 cr.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Construction work of Pune Ring Road project, spanning 169 km in length and costing INR 42,711 cr commenced.

---

| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | ~75 |
| Retail | ~125 |
| Residential | ~40 |

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft)* |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | ~11,000 |
| Retail | ~14,000 |
| Residential | ~12,000 |

**Caption:** Capital value (INR/sq ft)*. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.

---

### City highlights
*   Della Resorts formed a strategic partnership with Hiranandani and Krisala Developers to develop a 40-acre township project. This collaboration is projected to generate revenue of approximately INR 11 bn.

Chunk Content: # JLL SEE A BRIGHTER WAY

### Research author
**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS
+91 22 6620 7575 / +91 98198 65516
samantak.das@jll.com

### About JLL
For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $23.4 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 112,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. Driven by our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world, we help our clients, people and communities SEE A BRIGHTER WAY^SM.
